examples.title: Algorithm: Elliptic Curve Point Addition
examples.description: Adds two points on an elliptic curve over finite field
examples.code:
FUNCTION ec_point_add(P, Q, a, p)
BEGIN
    IF P == O:
        RETURN Q
    IF Q == O:
        RETURN P
    IF P.x == Q.x AND P.y != Q.y:
        RETURN O
    IF P != Q:
        m = (Q.y - P.y) * mod_inverse(Q.x - P.x, p) MOD p
    ELSE:
        m = (3*P.x^2 + a) * mod_inverse(2*P.y, p) MOD p
    x_r = (m^2 - P.x - Q.x) MOD p
    y_r = (m*(P.x - x_r) - P.y) MOD p
    RETURN (x_r, y_r)
END
examples.explanation: Handles special cases (identity element, vertical line), computes slope differently for addition/doubling, calculates resulting point coordinates using curve equation

examples.title: Algorithm: ECC Key Pair Generation
examples.description: Generates public/private key pair using elliptic curve cryptography
examples.code:
FUNCTION generate_ecc_keypair(curve)
BEGIN
    n = curve.order
    d = random(1, n-1)
    Q = scalar_multiply(d, curve.G)
    RETURN (d, Q)
END
examples.explanation: Selects private key 'd' randomly from [1, n-1], computes public key Q by multiplying base point G with d using scalar multiplication

examples.title: Algorithm: ECDSA Signature Generation
examples.code:
FUNCTION ecdsa_sign(message, d, n, G)
BEGIN
    k = random(1, n-1)
    (x1, _) = scalar_multiply(k, G)
    r = x1 MOD n
    IF r == 0: restart
    e = hash(message)
    s = (mod_inverse(k, n) * (e + d*r)) MOD n
    IF s == 0: restart
    RETURN (r, s)
END
examples.explanation: Generates random nonce k, computes signature components r and s using modular arithmetic and elliptic curve operations

examples.title: Algorithm: ECC Encryption (ElGamal variant)
examples.description: Encrypts message point using recipient's public key
examples.code:
FUNCTION ecc_encrypt(M, Q, G, n)
BEGIN
    k = random(1, n-1)
    C1 = scalar_multiply(k, G)
    S = scalar_multiply(k, Q)
    C2 = point_add(M, S)
    RETURN (C1, C2)
END
examples.explanation: Uses ephemeral key k to create ciphertext components. Security relies on elliptic curve discrete logarithm problem
